cur_source_name: Brazil Ministry of Health dTpa page; 2024 national schedule instruction; Ministry pages for maternal dTpa use
cur_notes: Brazil retains whole-cell DTP and DTP-Hib-HepB primary and childhood booster products in the public schedule while also using adult acellular dTpa in pregnancy; Ministry of Health materials state that gestational dTpa has been offered since 2014, so country-level PRN exposure is coded mixed from 2014 onward.

cur_source_name: China CDC pertussis vaccine immunoprophylaxis page; Shenzhen Kangtai DTaP-Hib-IPV product page; recent China vaccine genomics and review papers
cur_notes: China CDC states that DTaP had completely replaced DTwP by 2012. Current Chinese acellular products and recent reviews indicate heterogeneous antigen composition, including PRN-positive component or co-purified vaccines and PRN-negative two-component vaccines, so post-2012 country-level PRN exposure is curated as mixed rather than uniformly PRN-positive.

cur_source_name: Czech 2018 hexavaccine recommendation; EMA Infanrix hexa
cur_notes: Czech expert guidance states that from 1 January 2018 only Hexacima would be routinely available, while children already started on Infanrix hexa in 2017 should complete the same schedule with Infanrix hexa when possible; the pre-shift 2007-2017 routine hexavalent period is therefore curated as PRN-positive Infanrix-hexa-type exposure

cur_source_name: Spanish pertactin-deficiency study
cur_notes: The Spain-specific PRN-deficiency study reports approval in 2013 of a vaccine without PRN for primary vaccination in 5 of 19 regions, so the later national programme is curated as heterogeneous and mixed rather than cleanly PRN-free.

cur_source_name: French Bordetella evolution study; French 2024 vaccination calendar; EMA Infanrix Hexa; EMA Hexyon; Merck VAXELIS PI
cur_notes: Acellular pertussis was introduced in 1998 as an adolescent booster and then generalized to the whole population; French acellular products include both PT/FHA-only and PT/FHA/PRN formulations, so the programme is curated as mixed-brand heterogeneous rather than uniformly PRN-positive.

cur_source_name: UKHSA hexavalent programme guidance; GOV.UK pertussis vaccine use; EMA Infanrix Hexa; Merck VAXELIS PI
cur_notes: UK infant programme uses Infanrix hexa or Vaxelis and maternal or booster products are acellular pertussis vaccines with PRN-positive formulations

cur_source_name: PAHO and MSPAS Guatemala vaccination dashboard launch; WHO-prequalified DTwP-HepB-Hib package insert; PAHO notice on Guatemala hexavalent introduction
cur_notes: PAHO and MSPAS identify Pentavalente and DPT as routine childhood pertussis-containing vaccines in Guatemala, and the November 2025 PAHO and MSPAS launch of hexavalent indicates that this switch occurred after the analytic GTM row in 2014. The WHO-prequalified DTwP-HepB-Hib package insert specifies whole-cell pertussis vaccine in the pentavalent class, so the pre-hexavalent public schedule is curated as whole-cell and PRN-unexposed with high-confidence class-level support

cur_source_name: India Immunization Handbook for Medical Officers 2017; CDSCO DTwP Biological E SmPC; CDSCO Liquid Pentavalent (Bio E) SmPC
cur_notes: The UIP handbook schedules pentavalent primary doses and DPT boosters in the public programme, and CDSCO SmPCs identify both DTwP and liquid pentavalent DTwP-rHepB-Hib products as whole-cell pertussis vaccines; the routine public childhood series is therefore curated as whole-cell and PRN-unexposed
